notes: "Mount Zion and . . . punish . . . the king of Assyria." The Assyrians will attack Mount Zion, the hill upon which Solomon built his temple. (1 Kings 8:1.) (This attack occurred in 701 b.c., under the Assyrian king Sennacherib.) Yet the Lord will punish the king of Assyria for his "stout heart" and "high looks"

"It has already been observed that Solomon deferred the dedication of the temple to the following year after it was finished, because that year, according to Archbishop Usher, was a jubilee." (Clarke)
Ethanim "enduring" seventh Jewish month, corresponding to modern Oct. to Nov.; so named because permanent streams still flowed
In the end there is nothing but the reminder or token of the covenant left in the ark.
"It is generally agreed that there were now two tabernacles, one at Gibeon, and the other in the city of David, which one David had constructed as a temporary residence for the ark." (Clarke)
Solomon is pointing both of his hands towards the heavenly throne in making an oath before heavenly witnesses (see 1 Kings 8:54).

quotes.text: The Lord uses the weather sometimes to discipline his people for the violation of his laws. He said to the children of Israel:
quotes.speaker: Spencer W. Kimball
quotes.attr: Spencer W. Kimball, in Conference Report, Apr. 1977, 4; or Ensign, May 1977, 4
